meta.subject: Literature in English
meta.philosophy: This syllabus is designed to guide the teaching and learning of Literature in English at the Ordinary Level, from Form 1 to 4. It develops critical thinking, analytical abilities, self-awareness, and deep appreciation for Literature. By engaging in a wide range of literary works, learners acquire important skills such as interpretation, empathy, and communication, as well as attitudes that promote open-mindedness.
meta.competence_learning: The syllabus employs a competence-based approach which emphasises hands-on activities. Learners are involved in the creation of various literary works, showcasing their understanding and mastery of literary concepts and techniques. They acquire both theoretical knowledge and practical skills, enabling them to analyse and produce Literature with confidence. Suggested methodologies include teacher exposition, class discussions, group work, simulation, dramatisation, project-based learning and multimedia integration. At Form 2, text study and deeper genre analysis are introduced.
goals: Introduce learners to Zambian Literature and its development.
Develop understanding of genres: Prose, Drama, and Poetry.
Foster critical analysis of literary elements in fiction and non-fiction.
Develop skills to compose and perform literary works.
Nurture appreciation of both Zambian and global literary contexts.

specific_outcomes: 2.1.1.1.1 Explore the Development of Zambian Literature
2.1.1.1.2 Show understanding of different Zambian Oral Traditions
2.1.1.2.1 Apply Themes from Zambian Literature in real life situations
2.1.1.3.1 Explore Prominent Zambian Authors, playwrights and poets
2.2.1.1.1 Demonstrate understanding of Fiction and Non-Fiction
2.2.1.2.1 Demonstrate understanding of Elements of Prose
2.2.1.2.6 Analyse text for point of view
2.2.2.1.1 Demonstrate understanding of different types of drama
2.2.2.2.1 Demonstrate understanding of elements of drama
2.2.3.2.1 Demonstrate understanding of elements of poetry
2.2.3.2.3 Use metaphors in different linguistic contexts
